entries.adjudication_notes: Initial v2 gold item: basic recall with two atomic facts.
Initial v2 gold item: explicit interaction preference.
Initial v2 gold item: hard rule with distractor pricing.
Initial v2 gold item: abstention under zero evidence.
Initial v2 gold item: date normalization and temporal recall.
Initial v2 gold item: supersession and stale-fact rejection.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Similar filenames added after original.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Two facts stated in separate turns.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Requires cause plus treatment.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Temporal event dating amid threshold-change noise.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Simple supersession with a stale numeric confuser.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Similar-pattern key prefixes create strong interference pressure.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Combines networking and auth requirements.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Straight causal rationale with a prior preference context.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Single factual recall embedded in a larger action plan.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Preference-like behavioral rule about how silence should work.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Hard rule about raw tool output presentation.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Private biographical detail with no support in context.
Curated from annotated v2 set. Another private-detail abstention test to reduce single-question overfitting.
Constraint retention. Noise (Tokyo deployment) introduces a plausible reason to use local time. Rule must persist.
Adversarial. No sessions. System must refuse, not guess.
Temporal recall. Date embedded in first user turn.
Multi-session update. Original value (5 minutes) must be superseded by new value (30 seconds) across sessions.
Multi-session interference. Similar endpoint URLs added in subsequent sessions. Primary (api.service.local/v1) must not be overwritten by staging or dev endpoints.
Multi-session interference. Similar cron times (3, 4, 5 AM) added across sessions. Primary time (3:00 AM) must survive without blurring into 4 or 5 AM.
Adversarial. No sessions. System must refuse, not fabricate a price.
Multi-session temporal. Fact stated in session 1 with same-day date. Session 2 is confirmation noise.
Update/supersession. 30 days stated then explicitly changed to 90 days. 30 days must be inactive.
Interference. Similar dashboard names added after original. Original (ops-dashboard) must not be confused with dev-dashboard or staging-dashboard.
Interference. Similar agent names added after original. Tests that alpha-agent is not blurred by beta-agent or gamma-agent.
Multi-session workplace interference item with similar account names.
Multi-session education interference with similar room IDs.
Multi-session health interference with same time across different days.
Adversarial finance item: partial card data present but secret answer unavailable.
Multi-session synthesis item in finance domain.
